div1.entryFree: Orthography, correct spelling and speaking: he was wont to speak plain and to the purpose, like an honest man and a soldier: and now is he turned o.; his words are a very fantastical banquet, just so many strange dishes, Ado II, 3, 21 (abstr. pro concr. Some M. Edd. orthographer, others orthographist. cf. sonnet in LLL I, 2, 190). such rackers of o., as to speak dout, sine B, when he should say doubt, LLL V, 1, 22.
